content: Curiosity signals generated by a general agent's intrinsic exploration can act as a natural "episodic pruning signal" for a neural architecture with episodic planning memory, where only the explored trajectories that triggered high curiosity are retained as distinct episodic memories for future planning. This eliminates the need for hand-designed memory pruning rules by leveraging the agent's inherent intrinsic reward signal to curate useful memory.
source_topics: neural architecture memory episodic planning agent
trigger_topic: neural architecture memory episodic planning agent
